content: Rarity has always been a factor of "craftability", no?  The materials used to craft it are what qualifies it as being rare.  As these ships will be easy to pick off by larger ships and very hard to build, expect that they overall numbers will drop dramatically once ship destruction becomes live.  Ships like the X4 may have similar numbers, but replacement ships can be built easily to offset the numbers destroyed if not increase in numbers from the original allocation.

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content: Been a busy day, catching up. The only comment I can make on this at the moment is that we use significant placeholder data in the current state of game testing, and as such, our sensitivity to protecting any of that data is minimized. These are obviously not official releases from us, and I'd also caution against making any decisions based on the information presented in the videos. Still in the process of assessing with the team, and it's not inconceivable that some data can be scraped from the web, even in the future. Nevertheless, we have intentions of publishing guides in the future with transparency around a lot of the formulas so players are on equal footing. Particularly as we approach a v1 release.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
Got you fam. There will be many options, even HOSAM (stick and mouse). If we see robust bindings and tons of options for flight and weapons for gamepads and HOTAS/SAS, etc maybe decide what kind of Pilot you want to be. 

If you want to freight around or taxi/transport  then a HOTAS will be fine. If you want to get in the mix and melt faces? HOSAS with pedals (like the Saitek rudder pedals) would offer fine control and immersion. I just don’t know what will be supported or how “simy” they want SA to be. If we see a SC/Elite type of game….it will be a very steep learning curve for someone who has never played this kind of game. This is a binding suggestion pic  for Dual Sticks as an example. Sometimes I get the vibe the team wants a SC kind of game…but maybe not this much? Like a balance between SC and No Mans Sky? I don’t know. All we can do is wait and see. If it is deep….I think spending a ton of time practicing will be key. Like….a lot of practice.

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: There is a need for an asset that is " decent " at being a currency -accepted for transactions (at least for large transactions), yet  also exceptionally good at being money (a store of wealth), because it is secure, digital, hard, verifiable by all, and un-confiscatable.  To be  hard, it must (verifiablly by all) have little or no inflation a hard asset with a verifiable, programmed inflation  ( or disinflation) rate that becomes in short order less inflation than that of gold.   To be unconfiscatable, it must be possible to hold it in a way that makes it difficult to identify the holder of the asset.  Otherwise history has shown that as soon as hard assets become most useful, due to a near collapse in the economy, governments tend to confiscate the hard assets as they ( including the USA ) have done within the middle of the last C.  So, lack of KYC is essential to an asset playing this role.  If BTC loses the ability to be held without KYC, then BTC does not fill the role, of an asset that is secure, digital, hard, verifiable by all, and can not be confiscated....... because if there is KYC, then governments can Identify the holder and apply pressure and force of their law in order to ...confiscate it.  It is of little use as a "pristine asset" if it can be confiscated at will by a government, in similar fashion to if it were to become insecure, no longer digital, no longer "hard", not able to somewhat easily be verified by all.  It must have every one of these characteristics, and lacking in none of them, in order to be able to fill the role of ..... " the pristine asset".    If it loses this role, then another digital asset will be created, that will be secure, digital, hard, verifiable by all and, un-confiscatable.  ...... I am not a financial advisor and this is not advice.

author: Signal
content: Hi @MightyNightmare, our sentiment score is calculated by monitoring social platforms where Star Atlas is discussed and using a machine learning model to classify the conversation as positive or negative. We then compare the percent of the conversation that is positive to the percent of conversation is negative to come up with a net sentiment score. A perfect score is 5, meaning all the conversation is positive. A score of 4.93 tells us that the conversation coming out of the last 426 event, specifically around the Showroom, was overwhelmingly positive and is a testament to the amazing work our developer team put into creating the Showroom.

author: Signal
content: I prefer NSDA as a metric personally for how the project is advancing and coming along and what level of community adoption and engagement you are getting.  A lot cleaner and easier to see at the ground level / from a users perspective.

author: Signal
content: Unique daily active users and unique weekly active users would be cool too.  Total community hours logged in game, etc.

author: DarthData
content: I hear your point about "defending the herd". I think one of the most exciting aspects of the 426 event was the media coverage it received and the impact that had on our brand's reach. Our press release was picked up by over 300 media outlets and our Twitter account received some high profile mentions including one by Epic Games. Our sentiment tooling is able to pick up on that expanded reach. So while I'm sure some of the 4.93 sentiment comes from existing community support, we see quantitative evidence of our reach growing and that expanded audience reacting positively to our new product releases.
